question: For two evenly-matched teams on a neutral floor, what are the odds of leading (and winning) at the end of each quarter?
headline: Two even teams are a coin flip at tip; a lead is worth much more late than early — up 6 after Q1 wins ~66%, the same 6 after Q3 wins ~76%.
read: Even teams are rarely separated early — 6 in 10 games are within a possession-or-two (±6) after Q1 — and steadily pull apart. By the final buzzer only a third of even games finish within 6, and 44% finish 11+ apart (double-digit finals are the norm even between equals, because scoring noise compounds).
A lead is worth more the later you hold it: up 6 is worth ~66% after Q1, ~74% by half, ~76% after Q3 — same six points, very different security depending on how much clock is left.

Useful caveat for late leads: a naive random-walk model says an end-Q3 lead of 6 should be worth ~86%; reality is only 76%. WNBA comebacks happen more than a smooth-noise model predicts (fouling, 3-point variance, coasting give the trailing team fatter tails). Quote the empirical number, not the model, for late-game leads.
caveats: Even-team identification is via a between/within margin-variance decomposition, not a clean spread filter (the pbp pregame-spread column is a constant junk 2.5). The decomposition shows team strength contributes negligibly to quarter-grain margin variance, so this is a small approximation, not a filtered even-game sample.

The conditional win-prob is pooled over all games (mix of even and mismatched). Conditioning on the current lead absorbs most strength information, but a leader is on average slightly the stronger team, which nudges the early empirical win% a hair above a pure even-team value.

Neutral court: home-court advantage is symmetrized out. In the real data home teams win 55.5% (avg margin +2.1). If one even team is at home, treat it as ~+2 pts / ~+5 pp ahead of the neutral numbers.
No player / injury / rest context — this is the league-average even matchup, the ground-floor baseline to reason from.

provenance.note: Every number reproducible by re-running the script. Values materialized from the finalized CP-C spec (docs/reports/DEEPDIVE_QUARTER_SCORELINE_ODDS_20260717.md).

index_row.question: Even teams — odds of leading / winning at each quarter break?
index_row.headline: Coin flip at tip; a 6-pt lead is worth ~66% after Q1 but ~76% after Q3 — a lead is safer the later you hold it.
